basic_info.title: Final Fantasy XIII
basic_info.review: Final Fantasy XIII delivers a visually stunning and emotionally resonant experience, though its linear design and restrictive gameplay loop may alienate fans of open-world exploration. The combat system is a significant evolution, offering fast-paced, strategic depth.

alternative_names: ファイナルファンタジーXIII

play_today.platform: Xbox One X / Xbox Series X|S (Backward Compatible)
play_today.details: The enhanced version with 4K resolution, improved cutscenes, and better performance. Considered the definitive version by Digital Foundry.
PC version enhanced with the community-made FF13Fix mod that uncaps framerate, fixes stuttering, and improves performance. Best for customization.

recommended_patches.name: FF13Fix Mod
recommended_patches.description: Performance and bug fixes mod that removes frame pacer, uncaps framerate (optional), enables triple buffering, and fixes various technical issues.

recommended_patches.name: Ultrawide Fix
recommended_patches.description: Removes pillarboxing on ultrawide monitors for 21:9 displays.

recommended_patches.name: Console Button Prompts
recommended_patches.description: Replaces PC keyboard prompts with console-style button icons for better visual clarity.

play_today.details: The original console release with the lowest cutscene quality among the three major platforms. While it runs at a stable 30 FPS, it suffers from longer load times and lacks the graphical fidelity of the Xbox 360 version. It is only recommended for players who already own a PS3 and cannot access PC or Xbox.

optimal_settings: Enable Vulkan renderer for best performance and compatibility
Set Resolution Scale to 2x or 3x for sharp 1080p/4K visuals
Enable Async Shader Compilation to reduce stuttering during gameplay
Set CPU Thread Mode to MTU (Multi-Threaded Ultra) for smoother frame pacing
Enable Vulkan renderer for optimal Linux performance
Set Resolution Scale to 2x for balanced performance and clarity
Enable Async Shader Compilation to prevent frame drops
Configure GPU Driver Settings to use 'Force' for shader compilation

play_today.details: The best console option via Xbox One/Series X backwards compatibility. This version runs at a stable 30 FPS with significantly better cutscene quality than the PS3 version. It offers a plug-and-play experience with modern controller support and achievements, though it lacks the modding capabilities of the PC version.
Cloud streaming version released in Japan. Requires stable internet connection and offers the least control over performance settings.

The definitive modern experience via Steam. While the base game has performance issues, the community has created essential patches like FF13Fix that unlock 60 FPS, fix stuttering, and enable widescreen support. It offers the most convenient access with cloud saves, controller support, and the ability to mod the UI for better quality of life.

recommended_patches.name: FF13Fix
recommended_patches.description: Essential performance patch for the Windows version that fixes stuttering, unlocks 60 FPS, enables widescreen support, and resolves various graphical glitches.

recommended_patches.name: The FF XIII HD Project
recommended_patches.description: Comprehensive graphical overhaul for the Windows version that improves texture quality, character models, and environmental details for HD and UHD resolutions.

recommended_patches.name: FF XIII HD Full Motion Video - English
recommended_patches.description: Replaces the low-resolution pre-rendered cutscenes in the Windows version with AI-upscaled 4K resolution FMVs for a modern visual experience.

recommended_patches.name: Final Fantasy XIII Ultrawide Fix
recommended_patches.description: Removes pillarboxing (black bars) on ultrawide monitors for the Windows version, allowing the game to render correctly across the full screen width.

recommended_patches.name: HD Models Plus
recommended_patches.description: High-quality model swap mod for the Windows version that automatically updates character and enemy models to higher fidelity versions.

description.synopsis: In the world of Gran Pulse, humanity is threatened by the fal'Cie, god-like beings who control the fate of the world. Lightning, a soldier in the Cocoon military, is transformed into a l'Cie, a cursed individual tasked with a crystal mission to save the world or face crystallization. She must unite with other l'Cie to challenge the fal'Cie and uncover the truth behind the prophecy that threatens to destroy both Cocoon and Gran Pulse.
key_features: Command Synergy Battle (CSB) system with real-time action
Linear narrative structure with cinematic presentation
High-fidelity graphics utilizing the Crystal Tools engine
Character customization via Crystarium progression system
Seamless transition between exploration and combat
Focus on a fixed party of three characters
Environmental puzzles integrated into the linear path
Photo mode and high-resolution textures on PC version
legacy_and_impact: Final Fantasy XIII marked a bold departure for the series, embracing a linear, cinematic approach that polarized the fanbase but showcased the technical prowess of the PlayStation 3. It established the Fabula Nova Crystallis mythology and paved the way for the Lightning Saga, influencing the narrative direction of subsequent entries.
